purpose: Nutrition reference data for the weekly-meal-planner-weightloss skill. Used to calculate TDEE, set macro targets, and guide meal plan design.

harris_benedict_revised.name: Harris-Benedict (Revised, 1984)
harris_benedict_revised.use_when: General population; good accuracy for most adults
harris_benedict_revised.note: Multiply BMR by activity multiplier to get TDEE

mifflin_st_jeor.name: Mifflin-St Jeor (1990)
mifflin_st_jeor.use_when: Most accurate for modern populations; recommended default
mifflin_st_jeor.note: Multiply BMR by activity multiplier to get TDEE

katch_mcardle.name: Katch-McArdle
katch_mcardle.use_when: Best for users who know their body fat % (athletes, fitness-focused users)
katch_mcardle.note: Requires body fat % input. More accurate than weight-based formulas for athletic builds.

sedentary.label: Sedentary
sedentary.description: Desk job, little or no exercise

extra_active.label: Extra Active
extra_active.description: Hard exercise + physical job or 2x daily training

conservative.recommended_for: Sustainable long-term loss, minimal muscle loss, beginners

aggressive.recommended_for: Short-term push; only if TDEE is >2000 cal to avoid muscle loss

maximum.recommended_for: Medical supervision only; risk of muscle loss and metabolic adaptation

safety_floor.note: Never go below these regardless of deficit calculation

balanced.label: Balanced / IIFYM
balanced.description: Flexible macro split. Good for general weight loss with moderate activity.

high_protein.label: High Protein
high_protein.description: Maximizes muscle retention during deficit. Best for people with strength training.

low_carb.label: Low Carb
low_carb.description: Reduces insulin response, helps some people control hunger. Not keto.

keto.label: Ketogenic
keto.description: Puts body in ketosis. Effective for some; requires strict adherence. Not ideal for high-intensity athletes.

mediterranean.label: Mediterranean
mediterranean.description: Emphasizes whole grains, olive oil, fish, legumes. Heart-healthy. Not the fastest fat loss approach.

vegan.label: Vegan / Plant-Based
vegan.description: No animal products. Requires attention to B12, iron, omega-3, zinc, and complete protein combining.

hunger_management_strategies.volume_eating: High-fiber, low-calorie foods fill stomach without using calorie budget (leafy greens, cucumber, broth-based soups)
hunger_management_strategies.protein_timing: Front-load protein at breakfast — reduces total calorie intake by 15-20% for most people
hunger_management_strategies.meal_frequency: 3 main meals with 1-2 snacks if needed — no strong evidence that frequency itself affects fat loss; pick what reduces hunger for you
hunger_management_strategies.hydration: 500ml water before meals reduces hunger and calorie intake by ~13% in studies

weight_loss_projection.formula: Expected weekly loss = daily_deficit / 3500 (lbs) or daily_deficit / 7700 (kg)
weight_loss_projection.note: Weight loss is not perfectly linear — expect fluctuations of ±2 lbs from water retention, glycogen, and food volume
weight_loss_projection.plateau_note: After 4–6 weeks, metabolism adapts. A 1–2 week diet break at TDEE can reset leptin and improve long-term results.
weight_loss_projection.muscle_loss_warning: Deficits >750 cal/day without resistance training increase muscle loss risk
